fact: Prefer Next.js Server Components by default. Use Client Components only when interactivity, browser APIs, or local UI state require them.
Keep privileged logic, auth checks, and database access on the server. Never trust client-provided role, hospitalId, or permission flags.
Validate all external input at the boundary with schema validation before using it in server actions, route handlers, or background jobs.
Use paginated queries for admin lists such as users, patients, appointments, and messages. Unbounded lists will degrade performance quickly in hospital operations.
Do not expose secrets or server-only modules inside Client Components. Keep environment-sensitive logic in server utilities.
File upload flows must validate mime type, file size, and access scope before creating attachment records or storage objects.
Realtime chat must enforce room membership and hospital scoping on the server side. Socket events must never rely solely on client claims.
Use consistent domain naming in the codebase: hospital, hospitalId, platform admin, hospital admin, patient, appointment, billing, lab, pharmacy.
